---
title: "PLACE"
subtitle: "**P**ersistence-**L**andmark **A**nalytic **C**lassification **E**ngine"
description: "A closed-form pipeline for certified point-cloud and graph classification."
author:
  - "Sushovan Majhi"
date: "2026-05-20"
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
footer: "PLACE  ·  S. Majhi  ·  ICERM TW-26-FCG  ·  Brown University  ·  20 May 2026"
---

# In This Edition {.section-divider}

## The Plan of the Talk {.section-divider}

::: {.edition-badge}
Vol. MMXXVI · No. III · Calcutta–Washington Edition · Six paise
:::

::: {.front-index data-title="Inside today's edition"}
| Page | Article |
|:----:|:--------|
|  I.  | Why placement matters — the puzzle motivating PLACE.                |
|  II. | The construction — local atlases from persistence.                  |
| III. | A theorem and its discontents.                                      |
|  IV. | Experiments — what the paper claims, and what it carefully does not.|
|   V. | Where this leaves us.                                               |

:::

::: {.composing-rule .crosses}
:::

# § I.  Motivation {.section-divider}

## The Puzzle

::: {.byline}
[§ I.]{.kicker} · [Motivation]{} · [Six minutes]{}
:::

A learned representation of a point cloud usually answers *what is here?* — class, label, embedding. It rarely answers *where in the shape is here?*

::: {.dispatches data-title="The gap PLACE addresses"}
- A vertex's coordinate vector says nothing about its **role** in the shape.
- Two combinatorially-identical patches at different curvatures look the same to a vanilla MLP.
- Persistence already encodes shape *globally* — but global summaries flatten location.
:::

## What we want

::: {.byline}
[§ I.]{.kicker} · [Motivation]{} · [Continued]{}
:::

::: {.standfirst}
A point-wise embedding that is *aware* of the topological neighbourhood it inhabits — a coordinate with a sense of place.
:::

::: {.fig-right}
![](assets/ornament.svg){width="220"}

*Fig. 1.* — A printer's flower, standing in for the figure your authors will provide on rendering.
:::

Three desiderata:

1. **Equivariance** under shape-preserving deformations.
2. **Discriminating power** for points distinguishable by local topology.
3. **Cheap to compute** at inference, even on graphs of millions of vertices.

The first two are theory questions; the third is engineering.

::: {.endmark .threefleur}
:::

## *A coordinate that knows its place must remember, at minimum, the birth-merge structure of the components around it — a histogram of distances cannot.* {.pull-quote}

::: {.attribution}
Lemma I.2 (informal)
:::

# § II.  Construction {.section-divider}

## Local atlas

::: {.byline}
[§ II.]{.kicker} · [Construction]{} · [Architecture A]{}
:::

::: {.standfirst}
For each point we cut a small ball, run a filtration on it, and read off a coordinate from the resulting persistence diagram.
:::

[Calcutta, 16 May —]{.dateline} The construction proceeds in three steps:

1. **Localise.**  Around vertex $v$, take a $k$-NN ball $B_k(v)$ of radius $r$.
2. **Filter.**  Build the alpha filtration on $B_k(v)$ up to scale $r$.
3. **Read.**  Extract the $H_0$ persistence diagram and embed it as a fixed-length vector $\Phi(v) \in \mathbb{R}^d$.

The resulting map $v \mapsto \Phi(v)$ is the **PLACE coordinate** of $v$.

## A picture is in order {.section-divider}

## The PLACE construction at a glance

::: {.plate data-plate="II"}
![](assets/ornament.svg)
:::

::: {.plate-caption}
**Pl. II.**  *The PLACE construction at a single vertex.*  Left: the local ball $B_k(v)$. Centre: the alpha filtration at three scales $r_1 < r_2 < r_3$. Right: the resulting $H_0$ diagram, embedded into $\mathbb{R}^d$ via the chosen vectorisation.
:::

## A theorem we can prove

::: {.byline}
[§ II.]{.kicker} · [Construction]{} · [Theory]{}
:::

::: {.marginalia data-title="Theorem II.1"}
Let $X, Y$ be two finite metric spaces, and let $f \colon X \to Y$ be an $\varepsilon$-isometry. Then for every $v \in X$ and every choice of vectorisation $\nu$ that is $L$-Lipschitz on $\mathcal{D}^{H_0}$,
$$
\bigl\| \Phi_\nu(v) - \Phi_\nu(f(v)) \bigr\|_2 \;\leq\; 2 L \varepsilon.
$$
:::

The constant is sharp: equality is attained on the unit-circle pair at $r = \pi/2$.

::: {.endmark}
:::

## A theorem we cannot

::: {.byline}
[§ II.]{.kicker} · [Construction]{} · [Theory, continued]{}
:::

::: {.standfirst}
The $H_0$-only variant is provably blind to certain $H_1$-distinguishable pairs. Architecture B addresses this — at a price.
:::

::: {.dispatches data-title="What we trade"}
- **Architecture A** ($H_0$ only):  cheap, Lipschitz-stable, blind to loops.
- **Architecture B** ($H_0 \oplus H_1$):  ~3× more expensive, captures cycle birth, no closed-form stability bound.
:::

The empirical question — does the loss of theory hurt? — is the subject of § III.

::: {.wire-stamp}
Wire · Persistent-Homology Desk · Calcutta
:::

# § III.  Experiments {.section-divider}

## Headline results

::: {.byline}
[§ III.]{.kicker} · [Experiments]{} · [Tabulated]{}
:::

::: {.stop-press}
Joint-4: 98.3 %
:::

| Dataset           | GNN baseline | + PLACE-A | + PLACE-B |
|-------------------|:------------:|:---------:|:---------:|
| MUTAG             |    87.4      |  90.1     | **91.7**  |
| NCI1              |    78.2      |  80.8     | **81.5**  |
| Joint-4 (synth.)  |    50.0      | **98.3**  |   98.4    |

: **Tab. I.** — Mean accuracy (%) over five seeds; bold marks the best per row. *Joint-4* is the angular-density discriminator described in § II; the $H_0$-only variant is sufficient.

::: {.errata data-title="Errata"}
Tab. I, MUTAG row: an earlier draft reported 89.4 for PLACE-A. Read **90.1** (corrected after re-running the seed).
:::

## *A 4-point gain on MUTAG is a 4-point gain on MUTAG. The 98.3 % on Joint-4 is a story about $H_0$.* {.pull-quote}

::: {.attribution}
authors' own caveat
:::

## A denser look

::: {.byline}
[§ III.]{.kicker} · [Experiments]{} · [In detail]{}
:::

::: {.handnote}
ask SM about Joint-4 ablations — does $H_1$ help at all here?
:::

::: {.broadsheet}
The headline numbers come from the same architecture, the same five seeds, and the same hold-out split as the published baseline. Differences are not from hyperparameter retuning — we deliberately froze every choice that could otherwise be argued to favour PLACE.

The Joint-4 result is the one that surprised us. Naive theory predicts 50% on this synthetic, because all four classes share the same global $H_1$ signature. Architecture A nevertheless reaches 98.3%, indicating that the *birth-merge structure* of $H_0$ is what carries the angular-density information — a finding we did not anticipate when we wrote § II.

The MUTAG and NCI1 results are more conventional. They confirm that adding a topological coordinate to a vanilla GNN improves classification by a few points, in line with the persistence-on-graphs literature.
:::

::: {.composing-rule .double}
:::

# § IV.  Looking Ahead {.section-divider}

## Open threads

::: {.byline}
[§ IV.]{.kicker} · [Future work]{} · [Three threads]{}
:::

::: {.dispatches data-title="What we leave open"}
- **PALACE.**  The same construction with a learned filtration. Differentiable; under review.
- **Stability for B.**  No closed-form bound; Wasserstein conjecture holds empirically.
- **Streaming PLACE.**  Constant-memory variant for online-graph settings.
:::

::: {.endmark .thirty}
:::

## Thank you {.colophon}

```{=html}
<div class="finis">F  I  N  I  S</div>
<svg class="wax-seal" viewBox="0 0 80 80" role="img" aria-label="Author's seal">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .seal-ring  { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 1.3; }
      .seal-inner { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.45; }
      .seal-mark  { fill: currentColor; stroke: none; }
      .seal-thin  { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 0.55; }
      .seal-mono  { font-family: 'Noto Serif Bengali', 'Bengali Sangam MN', 'Kalpurush', 'Vrinda', 'Lohit Bengali', serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 12px; fill: currentColor; }
      .seal-band  { font-family: 'Alegreya SC', 'EB Garamond', Georgia, serif; font-size: 4.6px; letter-spacing: 0.22em; fill: currentColor; }
    </style>
    <path id="seal-top"    d="M 11 40 A 29 29 0 0 1 69 40" />
    <path id="seal-bottom" d="M 11 40 A 29 29 0 0 0 69 40" />
  </defs>
  <circle class="seal-ring"  cx="40" cy="40" r="36" />
  <circle class="seal-inner" cx="40" cy="40" r="33" />
  <circle class="seal-inner" cx="40" cy="40" r="22" />
  <text class="seal-band">
    <textPath href="#seal-top" startOffset="50%" text-anchor="middle">SVSHOVAN  MAJHI</textPath>
  </text>
  <text class="seal-band">
    <textPath href="#seal-bottom" startOffset="50%" text-anchor="middle">·  ANNO  MMXXVI  ·</textPath>
  </text>
  <g>
    <polygon class="seal-mark" opacity="0.16" points="35,33 40,24 40,41" />
    <polygon class="seal-mark" opacity="0.16" points="40,24 45,33 40,41" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="40" y2="24" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="40" y1="24" x2="45" y2="33" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="40" y2="41" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="45" y1="33" x2="40" y2="41" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="40" y1="24" x2="40" y2="41" />
    <line class="seal-thin" x1="35" y1="33" x2="45" y2="33" />
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="35" cy="33" r="0.95" />
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="40" cy="24" r="0.95" />
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="45" cy="33" r="0.95" />
    <circle class="seal-mark" cx="40" cy="41" r="0.95" />
  </g>
  <text class="seal-mono" x="40" y="51" text-anchor="middle">সুশোভন</text>
  <g class="seal-mark">
    <circle cx="40" cy="56" r="0.6" />
    <circle cx="37" cy="59" r="0.6" />
    <circle cx="43" cy="59" r="0.6" />
  </g>
  <circle class="seal-mark" cx="9"  cy="40" r="0.95" />
  <circle class="seal-mark" cx="71" cy="40" r="0.95" />
  <g class="seal-mark">
    <circle cx="14" cy="14" r="0.55" />
    <circle cx="66" cy="14" r="0.55" />
    <circle cx="14" cy="66" r="0.55" />
    <circle cx="66" cy="66" r="0.55" />
  </g>
</svg>
```

::: {.contact}
[smajhi.com](https://smajhi.com) · [s.majhi@email.gwu.edu](mailto:s.majhi@email.gwu.edu) · Washington, D.C. · MMXXVI
:::

::: {.subscription}
Subscription: Six paise per fortnight. — Single copy: ½ anna. *Editorial offices:* Foggy Bottom · Salt Lake City. — Set in EB Garamond, Playfair Display, and Alegreya SC, on a sheet of aged paper rendered with Quarto and reveal.js.
:::
